verses.text: She wishes her beloved were as a brother she might openly embrace, and longs for his left hand under her head and his right embracing her. She craves unhindered nearness. The believer yearns for the closest, most open communion with Christ.

She wishes her beloved were as a brother she might openly embrace, and longs for his left hand under her head and his right embracing her. She craves unhindered nearness. The believer yearns for the closest, most open communion with Christ.

She wishes her beloved were as a brother she might openly embrace, and longs for his left hand under her head and his right embracing her. She craves unhindered nearness. The believer yearns for the closest, most open communion with Christ.

She charges the daughters not to stir love before its time, and asks, 'Who is this that cometh up from the wilderness, leaning upon her beloved?' The bride comes home supported by him. So the soul journeys through this world leaning wholly on Christ.

She charges the daughters not to stir love before its time, and asks, 'Who is this that cometh up from the wilderness, leaning upon her beloved?' The bride comes home supported by him. So the soul journeys through this world leaning wholly on Christ.

'Set me as a seal upon thine heart... for love is strong as death,' and 'many waters cannot quench love.' Here love reaches its height. The love between Christ and His own is unquenchable, stronger than death, and cannot be bought or drowned.

'Set me as a seal upon thine heart... for love is strong as death,' and 'many waters cannot quench love.' Here love reaches its height. The love between Christ and His own is unquenchable, stronger than death, and cannot be bought or drowned.
Care is taken for a 'little sister' not yet grown, to build and protect her. The strong watch over the weak. The Church tends her younger and weaker members, guarding them until they are established.
Care is taken for a 'little sister' not yet grown, to build and protect her. The strong watch over the weak. The Church tends her younger and weaker members, guarding them until they are established.

She is now 'a wall' with towers, one who 'found favour' in his eyes; the vineyard is let out, but hers she keeps for him. Maturity brings peace and fruitfulness. The settled believer keeps her heart's vineyard for Christ alone.

She is now 'a wall' with towers, one who 'found favour' in his eyes; the vineyard is let out, but hers she keeps for him. Maturity brings peace and fruitfulness. The settled believer keeps her heart's vineyard for Christ alone.

She is now 'a wall' with towers, one who 'found favour' in his eyes; the vineyard is let out, but hers she keeps for him. Maturity brings peace and fruitfulness. The settled believer keeps her heart's vineyard for Christ alone.

He asks to hear her voice, and she cries, 'Make haste, my beloved,' longing for his swift return. The Song ends in yearning. So the Church closes her praise with the cry, 'Come, Lord Jesus,' longing for His appearing.

He asks to hear her voice, and she cries, 'Make haste, my beloved,' longing for his swift return. The Song ends in yearning. So the Church closes her praise with the cry, 'Come, Lord Jesus,' longing for His appearing.
